sections.heading: Concept
sections.content: This is the table that shows the cumulative frequency of each of the classes and it is the running total of the frequencies class by class, giving the total frequency.

sections.heading: Examples and Practice
sections.content:
EXAMPLE: In a mock examination for the final year Chemistry class, the following were obtained by 50 students.
71 63 70 45 59 82 61 79 37 89
33 56 39 42 64 73 59 67 72 60
46 36 61 87 91 67 54 72 39 43
57 65 45 52 35 46 64 37 95 86
76 73 67 71 74 82 61 59 58 43
Using class interval 31 – 40, 41 – 50 ... e.t.c Construct a table showing the following columns: class interval, class boundary, class mark, frequency and cumulative frequency.
Class interval | Frequency | Class boundary | Class mark | Cumulative Frequency
31 – 40 | 6 | 30.5 – 40.5 | 35.5 | 6
41 – 50 | 9 | 40.5 – 50.5 | 45.5 | 6 + 9 = 15
51 – 60 | 9 | 50.5 – 60.5 | 55.5 | 9 + 15 = 24
61 – 70 | 11 | 60.5 – 70.5 | 65.5 | 11 + 24 = 35
71 – 80 | 9 | 70.5 – 80.5 | 75.5 | 9 + 35 = 44
81 – 90 | 4 | 80.5 – 90.5 | 85.5 | 4 + 44 = 48
91 – 100 | 2 | 90.5 – 100.5 | 95.5 | 2 + 48 = 50
